method_atom: A statistical-geometry method for an ensemble of closed two-dimensional boundary surfaces that enclose connected clusters: each surface is classified by its topological genus and its area, and the count of surfaces of given genus and area is found to follow a power law in area times an exponential decay, where the exponential rate is independent of genus and the power-law exponent is approximately linear in genus. The genus-summed count scaling as a power of area, together with a linear proportionality between enclosed volume and boundary area, is used to diagnose a branching instability of the surface ensemble, and the scaling exponents are tested for universality across distinct underlying discretizations.
object_atom: The interface surfaces bounding critical connected clusters of a three-dimensional nearest-neighbor Ising spin system at its critical temperature, together with the ordinary spin clusters at the minority percolation point and three-dimensional bond-percolation clusters, evaluated on simple-cubic and body-centered-cubic lattices.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the genus-and-area classification of an ensemble of cluster-boundary surfaces with power-law-times-exponential scaling diagnosing a branching instability; the domain-specific part is the 3d Ising/percolation spin-cluster interfaces on cubic lattices.
